errors.message: The 'outcomes' array only defines 4 named branches (authentic-repair, guarded-thaw, popularity-cost, avoidance-spiral), but the branching tree has 16 possible leaf paths (4 binary choice points) that resolve into only 4 ending scenes (friendship, tentative, lonely-win, broken). Most paths reaching scene-7-resolution-tentative, scene-7-resolution-lonely-win, and scene-7-resolution-broken do NOT match any outcome's 'triggeredBy' list. For example, reaching scene-6-showdown-strained -> choice-4b:'a' -> scene-7-resolution-tentative is a fully valid, reachable path, but no outcome entry has triggeredBy = [...,'choice-4b:a'] ending in tentative from that route (only choice-4b:b -> lonely-win is covered, and only for one specific upstream history).
errors.expectedValue: An outcome entry (or clearly defined fallback logic) covering every reachable choice-history/ending-scene combination, or documentation that outcome-matching degrades gracefully to a default branch
errors.actualValue: Only 4 of ~16 reachable paths have matching outcome definitions
errors.fix: Either add outcome entries for all reachable path permutations that terminate in scene-7-resolution-tentative / lonely-win / broken, or restructure 'triggeredBy' matching to key off which ending scene was reached (with sub-variants) rather than an exact full choice-history array, and document that behavior.

errors.message: branchDialogue contains an entry with branchId 'ending-tentative-ground' that does not correspond to any id in the top-level 'outcomes' array (valid ids are branch-authentic-repair, branch-guarded-thaw, branch-popularity-cost, branch-avoidance-spiral). Per the data model, branchDialogue variants are keyed off outcome branch ids, so this entry is orphaned and will never be selected by any outcome-matching logic.
errors.expectedValue: branchId matching an existing entry in outcomes[].id
errors.fix: Add a corresponding outcome object with id 'ending-tentative-ground' (or 'branch-tentative-ground') to the outcomes array, including a description and triggeredBy list covering the choice combinations that lead to scene-7-resolution-tentative, or rename this branchDialogue entry to match an existing outcome id.

errors.message: The branchDialogue entry for 'ending-tentative-ground' uses a different internal shape than all other branchDialogue entries — it has 'endingTitle' and 'flavorLines' fields instead of the 'lines' field used everywhere else, with no 'sceneId'/'branchId' pairing consistent with the other four entries' structure (those use {sceneId, branchId, lines: [...]}).
errors.expectedValue: { sceneId, branchId, lines: [...] } matching the shape of branchDialogue[0..3]
errors.fix: Normalize this entry to the same schema as the other branchDialogue entries (rename 'flavorLines' to 'lines', drop or standardize 'endingTitle' across all entries if it's meant to be a general field).

warnings.message: choice-3b option 'b' (deflect) maps three traits simultaneously (EMOTIONAL_AWARENESS:-2, INTEGRITY:-2, COMMUNICATION:-1), which is a large combined negative swing for a single choice compared to other choices in the episode that typically move 1-2 traits by 1-2 points.
warnings.suggestion: Consider reducing to two trait dimensions or smaller magnitudes to keep trait swings consistent with the rest of the episode's calibration.

warnings.message: Scene s6f-2 dialogue has an odd 'character: jordan, text: "", emotion: laughs early, then catches Alex's face, stops' entry with empty text but populated action field in scene-6-showdown-solid (s6s-3) as well — an empty text string for a speaking character line reads awkwardly in a dialogue renderer that expects non-empty text per line.
warnings.suggestion: Either give jordan a short verbal beat (e.g., '...') or restructure this as a pure NARRATOR/action-only beat instead of attributing an empty string to a named character.

warnings.message: Multiple distinct upstream choice histories converge on the same scene-6 variants (e.g., scene-6-showdown-strained is reachable both from the 'solid' library path via choice-3:b and from the 'cold' library path via choice-3b:a), which is intentional design but means the emotional-beat framing text in scene-6-showdown-strained ('the trust between Wren and Alex is thinner this time') may not perfectly fit both entry contexts.
warnings.suggestion: Confirm the description reads naturally regardless of which upstream path led here, or split into two convergent-but-distinct scene variants if the emotional context differs meaningfully.

warnings.message: estimatedPlayTime of 12 minutes seems low for an episode with 15 scenes, 8 choices, and multiple full branches of dialogue — likely fine for a single playthrough but worth double-checking against actual dialogue line counts.
warnings.suggestion: Verify against a timed read-through; consider revising if average playthrough (single path) exceeds 12 minutes.

recommendations: Rework the outcome-matching system so every reachable ending scene has full coverage of the choice-history permutations that lead there — either via exhaustive triggeredBy lists or via ending-scene-keyed matching rather than full-history matching.
Normalize the branchDialogue schema so all entries use the same field names (lines vs flavorLines, presence/absence of endingTitle).
Add an explicit outcome entry for the 'tentative' ending state so it has parity with friendship/lonely-win/broken outcomes for analytics and dialogue-branch resolution.
Consider a QA pass that walks all 16 possible choice-path permutations end-to-end to confirm each resolves to a coherent narration + matching outcome/branch dialogue.
